scenario_title: diagnosing a wheat crop's yellowing symptoms
scenario_description: Agriculture: diagnosing a wheat crop's yellowing symptoms

turns.text: I noticed these yellow streaks on my wheat leaves. Can you help me figure out what's wrong?
turns.image_description: A close-up of wheat leaves with pale yellow streaks along the veins, backlit by midday sun, with no visible pests or insects

turns.text: Based on the yellowing pattern along the veins, this could indicate a nutrient deficiency—possibly nitrogen or magnesium. Would you mind checking the undersides of the leaves for any pests?
I looked closer and found these tiny green insects on the leaf undersides. What do you think now?
turns.image_description: A lower-angle shot of the same wheat stalks, revealing tiny green aphids clustered on the undersides of leaves, with slight curling at the leaf edges

turns.text:
What is the primary cause of the wheat crop's yellowing symptoms and leaf damage?

A) Aphid infestation with secondary fungal infection
B) Nitrogen deficiency
C) Fungal infection alone
D) Environmental stress from drought

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 image showed vein-patterned yellowing, leading to initial nutrient deficiency hypothesis. Turn 2 image revealed aphids on leaf undersides, shifting diagnosis to pest damage. Turn 3 image confirmed aphid excrement and mold, establishing secondary infection.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The pest presence is only visible in the second image; initial symptoms alone are consistent with both nutrient deficiency and pest damage

_meta.scenario: Agriculture: diagnosing a wheat crop's yellowing symptoms
